data.title: Sanofi (SNY) Acquires Dynavax in $2.2 Billion Deal after FDA Rebuff
data.description:
Sanofi (SNY) said it will buy Dynavax Technologies (DVAX) for about $2.2 billion in cash to grow its vaccines business. The French drugmaker will pay $15.50 per share, representing a 39% premium to Dynavax's closing price on Tuesday. The deal adds Dynavax's hepatitis B vaccine, already sold in the U.S., and a shingles shot that is still in early testing.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Through this purchase, Sanofi aims to expand its reach in adult immunization, which includes vaccines for flu, RSV, meningitis, and pertussis. The company said the transaction will not affect its 2025 financial outlook and expects the deal to close in the first quarter of 2026.

Meanwhile, SNY shares rose 0.60% on Tuesday, closing at $48.32.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: BP to Offload 65% Stake in Castrol to Stonepeak for $6B
data.description:
Energy giant BP (BP) has struck an agreement to sell a 65% stake in its Castrol lubricant unit to Stonepeak for net proceeds of $6 billion. The sale proceeds from the transaction, which include $800 million for accelerated dividend payments, will be used to bring down the company's debt levels.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Here's Why BP Is Offloading Its Stake in Castrol

The sale of BP's 65% stake in Castrol will accelerate the company's efforts to streamline its business. Last week, BP announced the appointment of Woodside Energy's (WDS) Meg O'Neill as its next CEO, succeeding Murray Auchincloss. The company is targeting asset sales of $20 billion by 2027, with the aim of reducing its debt levels and cutting costs to improve profitability and share performance, which has lagged rivals like Exxon Mobil (XOM).

BP expects the sale of its 65% stake in Castrol to "significantly" bolster its balance sheet. The transaction valued Castrol at $10.1 billion with an implied EV/LTM EBITDA (enterprise value/last 12 months EBITDA) multiple of about 8.6x, reflecting the strength of the unit and its future growth potential.

The company added that upon completion of the sale, a new joint venture will be formed, with Stonepeak owning a 65% stake and BP holding the remaining 35%. BP believes that the retained stake will provide it with exposure to Castrol's growth in the years ahead. Notably, Castrol has delivered nine quarters of consecutive year-over-year earnings growth. BP will have the option to offload its remaining 35% stake in Castrol after the expiry of a two-year lock-up period.

Is BP Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Currently, Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on BP stock based on six Buys and three Holds. The average BP stock price target of $37.77 indicates 9.2% upside potential. BP's U.S.-listed shares have risen 17% year-to-date.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Why Is UiPath Stock (PATH) Soaring Today?
data.description:
UiPath (PATH) shares are soaring following the announcement that the company will be added to the S&P MidCap 400 index. UiPath has replaced Synovus Financial Corp. (SNV) in the index, reflecting the automation company's growing size and stronger market presence.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For context, UiPath is a leading automation software company that helps businesses automate repetitive tasks using robotic process automation (RPA) and AI.

What Does the Inclusion Mean for UiPath?

The S&P MidCap 400 Index tracks the performance of 400 mid-sized U.S. companies. Popular ETFs that follow this index include the SPDR S&P MidCap 400 ETF (MDY) and the iShares Core S&P Mid-Cap ETF (IJH).

Index inclusion often benefits companies by boosting visibility and increasing demand for their shares. For UiPath, being added to the S&P MidCap 400 could expand the company's investor base, as funds that track the S&P MidCap 400 will need to buy the stock. Over time, this may lead to higher trading activity, improved liquidity, and greater institutional ownership.

The change will take effect before the market opens on Friday, January 2, 2026.

Is UiPath a Good Stock to Buy?

Being added to the S&P MidCap 400 could improve UiPath's visibility and trading activity, but it doesn't change the near-term focus on revenue growth. Customer spending remains cautious, and there is still a risk that agentic products may contribute less than expected in fiscal 2026.

In the long term, UiPath could be a good buy for investors if there is continued growth in automation and enterprise software demand.

On Wall Street, analysts have a neutral stance on PATH stock. According to TipRanks, PATH stock has received a Hold consensus rating, with two Buys, 15 Holds, and one Sell assigned in the last three months. The average UiPath stock price target is $16.54, suggesting an upside of over 3% from the current level.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 12/24/25 – U.S. Stock Futures Hold Steady after S&P 500 Record High
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were down early Wednesday after the S&P 500 (SPX) hit a record high. The New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) will close early today at 1 p.m. EST for Christmas Eve and will remain closed on Thursday for Christmas Day.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.07%, 0.09%, and 0.10%, respectively, at 3:25 a.m. EST on December 24.

During Tuesday's regular trading, the three major indexes extended their winning streaks, led by big tech names such as Alphabet (GOOGL), Nvidia (NVDA), Broadcom (AVGO), and Amazon (AMZN). The S&P 500 increased 0.46% to close at a fresh high of 6,909.79, while the Nasdaq Composite and the Dow gained about 0.57% and 0.16%, respectively.

Looking ahead, traders will monitor the weekly initial jobless claims due Wednesday morning for fresh economic cues.

No key earnings reports are scheduled for today.

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.17%. WTI crude oil futures were trending higher, hovering near $58.62 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price increased to nearly $4,509 per ounce on Wednesday, hitting another record high.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened mostly higher on Wednesday as the year-end "Santa Claus rally" continued across global markets.

Asia-Pacific Markets Traded Mixed

Asia-Pacific markets traded mixed on Christmas Eve. Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index rose 0.17%. In China, the Shanghai Composite gained 0.53%, while the Shenzhen Component added 1.17%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei fell 0.14%, and the Topix declined 0.46%, after an early rally was muted following news that Japan planned to issue roughly 29.6 trillion yen in new government bonds to fund its fiscal 2026 budget.

data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Is the Stock Market Open Today, Christmas Eve?
data.description:
The U.S. stock market will be open half the day today, Wednesday, December 24, 2025, in honor of Christmas Eve. There will be an early close at 1:00 p.m.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Tomorrow, Thursday, December 25, the markets will be closed in honor of Christmas.

The markets will be open on Friday, December 26, the day after Christmas.

Keep track of all the stock market holidays with TipRanks' Stock Market Holidays calendar.

Will the Holiday Effect Impact the Markets This Week?

Understanding how the stock market might be affected by the holiday will help you invest well and beat the markets. Investors sometimes refer to the pre-holiday market rise and the post-holiday price drift as the "holiday effect."

The holiday effect is linked to investors' general optimism and confidence. The fact that customers tend to spend more money around the holidays is one explanation for the pre-holiday market surge. The share prices of retailers, in particular, may increase as a result.

As for the post-holiday price drift, one reason is that many traders and market analysts take time off work during the holidays to relax from their regular trading and investment research schedules. Those investors view the holidays as a time to unwind and refresh. Alternatively, some traders see the season as a chance to work harder and get some tactical advantages in the market, maybe by taking advantage of the period when many other traders take time off.

Read TipRanks News to learn about the Christmas holiday effect on the stock market. And remember to always use TipRanks to stay up-to-date on your stock research.
data.author: TipRanks Team

data.title: AMZN, MSFT, TCS: Judge Rejects Bid to Block Trump's $100,000 H-1B Fee
data.description:
A federal judge rejected a U.S. business group's challenge to overturn President Donald Trump's $100,000 fee on new H-1B visas for highly skilled foreign workers. The judge upheld the decision, ruling that the fee falls well within the president's broad powers to regulate immigration. The ruling is likely to impact technology and services companies the most, as they frequently hire specialized talent from overseas.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The U.S. Chamber of Commerce and the Association of American Universities argued that the administration lacked the legal authority to enforce the $100,000 H-1B visa fee. Among the top businesses lobbying against the decision were Amazon (AMZN), Microsoft (MSFT), Tata Consultancy (IN:TCS), Apple (AAPL), and Alphabet (GOOGL).

The Road Ahead for Skilled Workers

Daryl Joseffer, the Chamber's top lawyer, said the new fee could be unaffordable for many small and medium-sized businesses. He expressed disappointment and indicated potential further legal action to ensure the H-1B program aligns with Congress's goals. The H-1B program allows U.S. employers to recruit foreign workers with specialized training across diverse sectors. Amazon received the most H-1B visas, with over 10,000 approvals, followed by Tata Consultancy. Notably, California has the largest share of H-1B workers.

The H-1B program issues 65,000 visas annually, plus 20,000 more for individuals with advanced degrees. The visas are valid for three to six years. Trump's order would raise the cost of obtaining these visas from about $2,000 to $5,000 or higher, forcing employers to either pay substantially more for labor or hire fewer highly skilled foreign workers.

Meanwhile, the U.S. has overhauled the H-1B visa system, scrapping the lottery in favor of a weighted process that prioritizes higher-skilled, higher-paid workers. The new system takes effect February 27, 2026, for the next cap-registration season and includes a rule requiring employers to pay an extra $100,000 per visa.

Which Is the Best Technology Stock, According to Analysts?

We used the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool for Top Technology Stocks to determine which stock is most favored by analysts.

Currently, Wall Street has assigned a "Strong Buy" consensus rating on Adobe (ADBE), Mastercard (MA), Visa (V), Nvidia (NVDA), and Microsoft. NVDA offers the highest upside potential among them.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Lilly vs. Novo Nordisk: Who Will Lead India's Growing Obesity-Drug Market?
data.description:
U.S.-based Eli Lilly & Co. (LLY) and Danish drug maker Novo Nordisk (NVO) are vying to capture a large portion of India's rapidly growing obesity drug market. Lilly's Mounjaro competes with Novo's Ozempic and Wegovy as leading GLP-1 agonists. These drugs imitate a hormone that slows digestion and promotes a feeling of fullness, helping people eat less.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

India is estimated to have the second-largest overweight or obese population by 2050, making it a major focus for obesity drug makers. Analysts expect the global market for these drugs to reach about $150 billion per year by the end of this decade. The U.S. remains the largest market for obesity drugs, but India is showing rapid early sales, with most people in India paying for the medicine themselves.

The Obesity Drug Race Heats Up in India

Both companies are deploying different strategies to acquire customers in India before cheaper generic versions become available in March next year. Novo's plan focuses on cutting prices and bringing products to market faster, while Lilly has the first-mover advantage. Lilly's Mounjaro was launched in India in March 2025, while Wegovy was approved in June, both as diabetes and weight-loss drugs.

They are marketing aggressively to doctors, expanding obesity awareness, teaming up with clinics, offering patient incentives, and making distribution deals with local drugmakers, according to doctors, analysts, medical reps, patients, and distributors Reuters spoke with. Lilly has also partnered with famous Bollywood actors in India for a social-media ad campaign about obesity.

LLY vs. NVO: Current Status in India

Mounjaro has become the top-selling therapy by value in October, with sales doubling after its March launch. More than 20 Indian drugmakers plan to launch cheaper versions of Novo's weight-loss drug in India after its semaglutide patent expires in March 2026. Analysts expect generics to cost about 60% less, which could intensify competition, especially for Novo, in India's price-sensitive market.

Mounjaro is gaining traction in India, with its 2.5 mg Mounjaro KwikPen costing about Rs 13,125 ($146.79) for a month. In response, Novo reduced Wegovy's price in India by up to 37% in November, with the 0.25 mg dose priced at Rs 10,850 ($121.34) for a month. Ozempic launched last week at Rs 8,800 ($98.42) for a 0.25 mg monthly pack. Even if Lilly lowers prices later, Mounjaro would still cost about 30% more than other branded weight-loss drugs.

The Future of Obesity Drugs in India

Weight-loss treatments are reaching beyond India's cities to middle-class families and people with obesity-related health issues in smaller towns. Lilly is expanding in India by launching a second tirzepatide brand, Yurpeak, with Cipla ($IN:CIPLA) and working with Apollo Hospitals to raise obesity and diabetes awareness. Cipla will help market Lilly's drug in deeper markets, and Lilly is investing over $1 billion to expand manufacturing in India.

Novo is widening its distribution by partnering with Emcure ($IN:EMCURE) and launching a second semaglutide brand, Poviztra, for smaller markets. It has also teamed up with Healthify for health coaching and with Apollo to boost obesity awareness.

LLY or NVO: Which Is the Better Pharma Stock?

We used the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool to determine which pharmaceutical stock between the two is currently preferred by analysts.

Wall Street has assigned a Strong Buy consensus rating on LLY stock, with a nearly 6% upside potential over the next twelve months, despite a year-to-date gain of 39%.

By contrast, NVO carries a Moderate Buy rating with about a 7.1% upside, alongside a 44% decline in its stock price so far this year.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Nvidia, AMD Eye Billions in China AI Chip Windfall — But Who's the Real Winner?
data.description:
Recent reports pointing to a possible restart of GPU sales to China have raised questions about how much lost revenue Nvidia (NVDA) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) could recover if export approvals come through. In a new note, Raymond James said Nvidia stands to gain far more than AMD, even if approvals are limited, due to higher pricing and the larger scale of its data center business.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

AMD Sees Limited Upside from China Sales

Raymond James said AMD could add $500 million to $800 million in revenue over time if Chinese approvals come through, which would equal about $0.10 to $0.20 in non-GAAP EPS.

The outlook depends on China-approved accelerator orders, with reports suggesting Alibaba (BABA) could be a key buyer of 40,000 to 50,000 MI308 chips. At the midpoint, the firm said "a $15,000 chip would equate to about $675 million in sales," with that revenue spread over several quarters.

Raymond James added that approval timing, shipment pace, and possible limits on purchases of U.S.-made AI chips still remain uncertain.

Nvidia's China Upside Is Much Larger

For Nvidia, the numbers are far bigger. Raymond James said a limited return of China sales could add $7 billion to $12.5 billion in revenue, equal to roughly $0.15 to $0.30 in non-GAAP EPS in 2026.

The estimate assumes some resumption of H200 GPU shipments to large Chinese cloud and internet companies. At prices of $20,000 to $25,000 per unit, even smaller shipment volumes could generate billions in sales.

That said, the firm stressed that China remains a small slice of Nvidia's data center business. Growth continues to come mainly from U.S. hyperscalers, sovereign AI projects, and enterprise customers as new platforms roll out through 2026.

Which Is the Better AI Chip Stock?

We used the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool to see which AI chip stock Wall Street currently prefers.

Both Nvidia and AMD carry a Strong Buy consensus rating from analysts. Based on current price targets, analysts see slightly higher upside for Nvidia, with about 39% upside, compared with roughly 30% upside for AMD.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Cathie Wood's Christmas Trades: ARK Invest Bets on WeRide and CRISPR
data.description:
Cathie Wood's ARK Invest ETFs (exchange-traded funds) made notable portfolio adjustments ahead of Christmas, on December 23, as revealed in daily fund disclosures. The trades reflect ARK's continued focus on disruptive-innovation technologies such as biotech, autonomous driving, blockchain, and fintech, while trimming positions in areas showing slower growth.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The largest trade of the day was the purchase of 356,263 shares of Chinese autonomous driving technology company WeRide (WRD), totaling $3.19 million. Wood capitalized on the dip in WeRide shares, which declined by 1.1% yesterday.

Similarly, ARK increased exposure to gene-editing company CRISPR Therapeutics (CRSP), buying 43,333 shares for $2.50 million. CRSP stock also fell, by 2.5% yesterday.

Moreover, Wood added 333,370 shares of gene-sequencing company Pacific Biosciences (PACB). All of these buy trades follow substantial purchases in the past weeks, indicating Wood's growing interest in disruptive technologies.

Meanwhile, Wood made a single sale of 296 shares of Ibotta (IBTA), continuing with her gradual reduction of the mobile-technology company. IBTA shares were down 2.8% the previous day and have fallen almost 67% year to date.

Wood's Strategic Portfolio Shuffle

Let's see how these stocks perform using the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool:

Currently, analysts have a "Strong Buy" consensus rating on WeRide, with a 66.5% upside potential over the next twelve months. Meanwhile, they remain cautiously optimistic about CRISPR and Pacific Biosciences and keep a "Hold" on Ibotta shares.
